item.title: DMLS Alloy Selection: Matching Material to Application
item.description: The six DMLS alloys cover 95% of metal additive manufacturing applications. Choosing correctly at the design stage prevents expensive material substitution after build.

item.title: About Metal 3D Printing
item.description:
Metal Additive Manufacturing — No Compromise

Metal 3D Printing covers Direct Metal Laser Sintering and selective laser melting across six aerospace-grade alloys. Published by Ben Stagl (SAIC professor, 20-year fabrication veteran) and Max Davis (MAXCNC, digital fabrication specialist) — practitioners who have run production DMLS programs, not observers.

What We Cover

Process parameters, alloy metallurgy, design rules (wall thickness, overhang angles, support strategy), post-processing (stress relief, HIP, CNC hybrid), and supplier qualification. The depth that procurement engineers and materials scientists actually need.

item.title: Advertise on Metal 3D Printing
item.description:
Advertise on Metal 3D Printing

Metal 3D Printing reaches engineers and procurement managers actively sourcing metal additive manufacturing services, materials, and equipment.

Advertising Packages

Package Rate Placement
Banner Ad (728×90) $500/month Homepage
Sponsored Content $1,200/month Article feature
Network Package $3,500/month All PrintWire metal sites

Contact: maxdavisgroup@gmail.com

item.title: Services
item.description:
DMLS Metal Parts

Direct Metal Laser Sintering produces fully dense metal parts (99.5% theoretical density) from CAD files without tooling in 5–10 business days. Six certified alloys: Ti6Al4V (993–1,055 MPa), 17-4 PH (1,365–1,372 MPa), 316L (565–586 MPa), AlSi10Mg (268–345 MPa), Inconel 718 (958 MPa, stable to 700°C), CoCrMo (1,213–1,255 MPa). Build envelopes to 31.5" × 15.7" × 19.7".

Design for AM

DMLS enables topology-optimized geometry that machining cannot produce. Our DfAM service covers mass reduction (30–70% typical), internal channel design for conformal cooling, lattice structures (gyroid, BCC, TPMS) for lightweight applications, and support strategy to minimize post-processing cost. Free DFM analysis with every quote.
